chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّدَقَةِ لِبَنِي هَاشِمٍ وَ مَوَالِيهِمْ وَ صِلَتِهِمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 89 – The charity to the Clan of Hashim and their slaves and their connected ones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/190)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/190)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ أَ تَحِلُّ الصَّدَقَةُ لِبَنِي هَاشِمٍ فَقَالَ إِنَّمَا تِلْكَ الصَّدَقَةُ الْوَاجِبَةُ عَلَى النَّاسِ لَا تَحِلُّ لَنَا فَأَمَّا غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ فَلَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ وَ لَوْ كَانَ كَذَلِكَ مَا اسْتَطَاعُوا أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا إِلَى مَكَّةَ هَذِهِ الْمِيَاهُ عَامَّتُهَا صَدَقَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj, from Ja’far Bin Ibrahim Al Hashimy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Is the charity Permissible for the Clan of Hashim<sup>as</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But rather, that is the charity Obligated upon the people, it is not Permissible for us<sup>asws</sup>. So as for the other than that, so there is no problem with it. And had it been like that, they would not have the leeway that they go out to Makkah. These waters, the generality of it is charity’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Safwan ibn Yahya from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj from Ja’far ibn Ibrahim al-FIashimiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Is receiving charity lawful for the children of al- Hashim?’ He replied, ‘What is not lawful for us to receive of the people’s charity is obligatory charity. One is harmless when receiving charities other than obligatory. Had it been otherwise, they could not even go to Makkah; these waters are all charities.’”
